keyPoints: LSB encoding: The process of hiding data by modifying the least significant bits of each pixel in an image.
BPCS Steganography: A method of hiding data by modifying the bits of each pixel in an image based on the bit sequence of the text.
Lossless data hiding: The process of hiding data in an image without losing any information.
Applying steganography to images: Images can be used as a medium to hide data due to their high capacity.
Advantages of steganography in images: Images can be used to hide large amounts of data as they do not affect the quality of the image.
Disadvantages of steganography in images: Images can be detected using various techniques, making it difficult to hide data.
Common steganography techniques in images: LSB encoding, BPCS Steganography, DCT Steganography, FIBER Steganography.
Detecting steganography in images: Methods such as statistical analysis, entropy analysis, and machine learning can be used to detect hidden data.
